t_ch: 「球棒浣熊，他太能说了，这次我真的没法支持你了」
t_en: "Baseball Raccoon, he seems to know an awful lot. I really can't back you up this time."

t_ch: 一说起GDP，他面相都变了。比狸比狸还在吗？只有你能和他PK了……
t_en: His whole expression changes when we talked about GDP. Is Furblix still around? You're the only one who can take him on...

t_ch: 你的弱点要怎么击破？
t_en: How do I trigger a Weakness Break on you?

t_ch: …我的意思是，别光天天想着让人破防，你了解击破队吗？知道什么叫弱点击破和超击破吗？
t_en: ...What I'm trying to say is, stop obsessing over breaking people's composure all the time. Is your actual Break team comp complete? Have you wrapped your head around Weakness Break and Super Break yet?

t_ch: 我…我不知道…我的兴趣只有看《破晓战队》
t_en: I... I don't know... The only thing I'm into is watching Daybreak Squadron.

t_ch: 「是《破晓战队》系列某一部里的战斗机制，连我这样的的路人都知道」
t_en: "It's a combat mechanic from one of the Daybreak Squadron series. Even a casual like me knows that."

t_ch: 「你根本不是真的热爱《破晓战队》吧？*拖鞋*x3」
t_en: "You don't actually love Daybreak Squadron, do you? *Slipper* ×3"

t_ch: 我…其实我也不是每一部看了……
t_en: I... actually, I haven't seen every single one...

t_ch: 哇，他弱点太多了，完全被浣熊大人超击破了啊！
t_en: Whoa, look at all those weaknesses! He just got totally Super Broken by the Great Raccoon!

t_ch: 最终人气值PK结果，浣熊大人赢了哩！
t_en: And the winner of the popularity showdown is... the Great Raccoon!

t_ch: 胜败乃兵家常事…
t_en: Winning and losing are all part of the game...

t_ch: 没什么好骄傲的。
t_en: This is nothing to be proud of.

t_ch: 我输了…论人气，球棒浣熊确实更胜一筹……
t_en: I concede... When it comes to popularity, Baseball Raccoon really does have the upper hand...

t_ch: 破晓战队输给你是有原因的，这回我彻底服气了。
t_en: There's a reason Daybreak Squadron lost to you. I'm completely convinced this time.

t_ch: 与@破晓战队全肯定的直播PK就此落幕，你得到了二相乐园「民选直播之王」的称号
t_en: The livestream showdown against @Daybreak's No.1 Stan has concluded. You have earned the title of "People's Choice Streamer King."

t_ch: 胜败乃兵家常事…
t_en: Winning and losing are all part of the game...

t_ch: 最终人气值PK结果，呜呜，浣熊大人输了，怎么会这样哩？！
t_en: The final results of the popularity showdown are in... *sob*... The Great Raccoon lost! How could this even happen!?

t_ch: 胜败乃兵家常事…
t_en: Winning and losing are all part of the game...

t_ch: 还能重新来过吗？
t_en: Can we start over?

t_ch: 承认吧，这回是我赢了！
t_en: Just admit it, I'm the winner this time!

t_ch: 「呜呜你别欺负球棒浣熊，要欺负就冲着我来*火箭*x3」
t_en: "Heeey, leave Baseball Raccoon alone! Pick on me instead! *Rocket* ×3"

t_ch: 「有的人直播赢了，什么也没得到。有的人直播输了，却收获了我的心疼」
t_en: "Winning the showdown gets you nothing, but losing it wins my solicitude."

t_ch: 你…可恶！这虐粉的一套你也是学会了！
t_en: You... Grrr! You really know how to milk your fans for sympathy!

t_ch: 与@破晓战队全肯定的直播PK就此落幕，你得到了二相乐园「最窝囊社长」的称号
t_en: The livestream showdown against @Daybreak's No.1 Stan has concluded. You've earned the title of "Most Pathetic President" of Planarcadia.

t_ch: 好了，各位亲爱的朋友们，今天的直播PK就到这里哩！
t_en: Alright, dear friends, that's a wrap for today's livestream showdown!

t_ch: 额外声明，鉴于幸福微笑研究会在「星铁FES」展背后施展的小动作，以及对{NICKNAME}的名誉造成的影响，《狸狸周刊》宣布：主动终止与该组织的合作关系——
t_en: And a special announcement! Due to the Research Society of Happy Smiles' sneaky stunts at Star Rail FEST and the harm caused to {NICKNAME}'s reputation, Furbobo Weekly hereby terminates our partnership with them immediately!

t_ch: 我们<color=#dbc291ff>已将幸福微笑研究会从赞助商名单中划掉</color>，表明立场！
t_en: We <color=#dbc291ff>have officially crossed the Research Society of Happy Smiles off our list of sponsors</color> to make our stance clear!

t_ch: 按照惯例，我们还有一场征集网友热门问题的访谈，请大家持续关注《狸狸周刊》，所有采访内容都会发布在「乐园头条」栏目，我们稍后见！
t_en: As usual, we've got an interview featuring trending questions, so stay tuned to Furbobo Weekly! All interview content will be posted under Arcadia Alert. See you soon!

t_ch: 社长大人知道吗？《狸狸周刊》一直有一个地下传说：<color=#dbc291ff>狸猫们总是听见杂志社里半夜传来「沙、沙、沙的」走路声</color>……
t_en: Boss, did you know the ghost story here at Furbobo Weekly? They say furbos always hear the sound of scuffing footsteps coming from the office at midnight...

t_ch: 沙、沙、沙……
t_en: *scuff*, *scuff*, *scuff*...

t_ch: 听起来仿佛就在你背后，忽远忽近地跟着，但怎么也找不到来源……
t_en: It feels like it's right behind you, following close but always out of reach. You can never find where it's coming from...

t_ch: 沙、沙、沙…然后，大家发现…！
t_en: *scuff*, *scuff*, *scuff*... and then, everyone realized...

t_ch: 那「沙沙声」是一张会半夜飘来飘去、甚至会忽然贴到你背后的旧报纸！
t_en: That scuffing was actually an old newspaper that floats around at night and even sticks to your back!

t_ch: 这里是报社，有报纸不奇怪。
t_en: We're at a newspaper office. It's not that weird to have newspapers lying around.

t_ch: 然后呢，传说在这就结束了？
t_en: And then what? Is that the end of the story?

t_ch: 紧接着问题来了！在收拾公司的杂物时，我发现了一个不得了的东西——
t_en: But here's the kicker! While I was cleaning up some junk in the office, I found something incredible...

t_ch: 从我们文件柜夹缝里，突然掉出了一张*泛黄的报纸*。
t_en: A yellowed newspaper suddenly fell out from a gap in our filing cabinet.

t_ch: 我的狸毛还是一下子竖起来了哩，会不会它就是……
t_en: My fur stood right on end! Do you think it could be...

t_ch: 报纸形态的幻造种？
t_en: ...an imagenae in the form of a newspaper?

t_ch: 有…有可能哩！说不定是那种从来没有卖出去过，由「我好想被人看到啊」的怨念形成的幻造种哩。
t_en: I—It just might! Maybe it's the kind that never got sold and turned into an imagenae out of the sheer resentment of wanting to be read!

t_ch: 报警！是异常事件！
t_en: Call the police! It's an aberration!

t_ch: 我已经很怕了，社长大人别表现得比我更怕哩，一张旧报纸，还…还不至于惊动异常防御部吧……
t_en: Look, President, I'm already freaking out here, so don't you start acting even worse! It's just an old newspaper. I don't think we need to alert the Department of Aberration Defense just yet...

t_ch: 总之，为了解开真相，就请社长大人和小狸一起看看吧。
t_en: Anyway, let's solve this mystery together, Boss. Take a look with me!

t_ch: 旧报纸上写着难以辨认的二相乐园通用语，大概尤狸安会知道它们代表着什么含义。
t_en: The old newspaper is covered in illegible Planarcadia script. Joolyon might have an idea of what it says.

t_ch: 这张像是使用说明一样的小纸条，社长大人请看哩！
t_en: Please take a look at this little note, Boss! It's for instruction.

t_ch: 自带说明书？
t_en: Does it come with its own manual?

t_ch: 太贴心了吧！
t_en: How thoughtful!

t_ch: 好可疑，上面的墨水渍还没干，是谁现场写的吗？
t_en: Looks suspicious... the ink isn't even dry yet. Did someone just write this on the spot?

t_ch: 虽然不知道到底有没有用，如果社长大人准备好了的话，姑且按照它说的试一试吧。
t_en: I'm not sure if this will actually work, but if you're ready, let's give it a shot, President.

t_ch: 不知何处出现的小小尘灵，在旧报纸上一动不动，似乎在感受着你的注视……
t_en: A Tiny Wispae from who-knows-where sits perfectly still on the old newspaper, as if feeling your stare...

t_ch: 唤醒通灵报纸的咒语叫做…？
t_en: What's the incantation to awaken the seance newspaper again?

t_ch: 糊涂狸，狸糊涂，请您下凡。
t_en: Foolbribo, Foolbrito, please descend to us.

t_ch: 尘灵一动不动，似乎不是这个咒语？
t_en: The wispae doesn't move. Is that not the right incantation?

t_ch: 狐够够，胡狸狸，求您下凡。
t_en: Fullbrito, Furbribo, please descend to us.

t_ch: 步离狐，狐不理，请你下饭。
t_en: Boribo, Furbrezeto, please descend to us.

t_ch: 胡构狸，胡构狸，请您下凡。
t_en: Furbricato, Furbricato, please descend to us.

t_ch: …如果您出现了的话，请回答「是」。
t_en: If you're here, please answer "yes."

t_ch: 啊！真的召唤成功了，通灵报纸有感应了。
t_en: Ah! It worked! The seance newspaper is responding!

t_ch: 接下来，社长大人要不要试着让它回答一些简单的问题，如果觉得它可以信任，我们再继续深入提问哩——
t_en: Now, why don't you try asking some simple questions, Boss? If it seems trustworthy, we can dive deeper.

t_ch: 我是谁？
t_en: Who am I?

t_ch: 胡构狸，胡构狸，请问你知道我是谁吗？
t_en: Furbricato, Furbricato, do you know who I am?

t_ch: 它指向了「是」。这是知道的意思？
t_en: It's pointing to "Yes." I guess it knows?

t_ch: B-O-S-S，「BOSS」？！
t_en: B-O-S-S... "BOSS"!?

t_ch: 不得了，它竟然知道社长大人是《狸狸周刊》的老板哩！
t_en: No way! It actually knows you're the boss of Furbobo Weekly!

t_ch: 我旁边的狸猫是谁？
t_en: Who's the furbo next to me?

t_ch: 胡构狸，胡构狸，请问我旁边的狸猫是谁？
t_en: Furbricato, Furbricato, who is this furbo next to me?

t_ch: D-O-G，「DOG」？
t_en: D-O-G... "DOG"?

t_ch: 什、什么，你把我当成臭狗一样玩耍！我尤狸安是狗吗…？
t_en: Wh—What? You're treating me like some common mutt! Am I, Joolyon, a dog to you?

t_ch: 狸猫不是犬科吗？这个回答，好像也不算错……
t_en: Wait, aren't furbos technically canines? That answer isn't technically wrong...

t_ch: 你是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 胡构狸，胡构狸，请问你是谁？
t_en: Furbricato, Furbricato, who are you?

t_ch: 它指向了「否」。
t_en: It points to "No."

t_ch: 不知道自己是谁？还是说…你有什么不能回答的秘密哩？
t_en: It doesn't know who it is? Or... is it a secret you're not allowed to tell?

t_ch: 孤波算法怎么解？
t_en: How do you solve the solitary waves theory?

t_ch: 胡构狸，胡构狸，告诉我孤波算法的解法吧。
t_en: Furbricato, Furbricato, tell me the solution to the solitary waves theory.

t_ch: 社长大人还是放过它吧。
t_en: Boss, show some mercy.

t_ch: 看来有点灵验嘛！社长大人，也许现在正是探明旧报纸传说的时候哩！
t_en: Wow, it's actually working! Boss, this is our chance to finally crack the mystery of the old newspaper story!

t_ch: 不过这个灵异传闻，至少前社长时期就在流传了，为什么胡构狸突然显灵了哩？
t_en: But the story's been around since way before your time. Why is it anwsering our summon all of a sudden?

t_ch: 不如直接问它好了。
t_en: Why don't we just ask it directly?

t_ch: 你显灵有什么用意吗？
t_en: What made you want to answer us all of a sudden?

t_ch: 胡构狸，胡构狸，你突然显灵有什么用意吗？
t_en: Furbricato, Furbricato, what made you want to answer us all of a sudden?

t_ch: 它指向了「是」，但这个回答很模糊哩。
t_en: It's pointing to "Yes," but that's not exactly helpful.

t_ch: 我觉得，它应该是缺乏愿力了吧…想想也正常，被人忘记的幻造种就会缺乏愿力的供养，慢慢崩解成尘灵哩。
t_en: I think it's running low on Wishpower... It makes sense. Any imagenae forgotten by the world loses its source of Wishpower and slowly starts to disintegrate into wispae.

t_ch: 你能被我们祛除吗？
t_en: Can we vanquish you?

t_ch: 胡构狸，胡构狸，如果你是怨灵，你能被我们祛除吗？
t_en: Furbricato, Furbricato, if you're a ghost, can we vanquish you?

t_ch: 不、不能祛除？！
t_en: N-No? We can't!?

t_ch: 胡构狸，胡构狸，我们送你回家吧…你的家在哪里？
t_en: Furbricato, Furbricato, let's get you home... Tell us, where do you live?

t_ch: H-E-R-E，「HERE」？
t_en: H-E-R-E... "HERE"?

t_ch: 这里？！
t_en: Here!?

t_ch: 哩啊，怎么会？你是《狸狸周刊》的地缚灵？请灵容易送灵难，这下真得找异常防御部帮忙哩。
t_en: Huh, no way! You're a bound spirit of Furbobo Weekly? Ugh, they say it's easier to invite a ghost than to kick one out. We're gonna have to call the Department of Aberration Defense for sure!

t_ch: 我的恋爱运势怎么样？
t_en: What's my love life looking like?

t_ch: 胡构狸，胡构狸，我的恋爱运势是不是不错？
t_en: Furbricato, Furbricato, is my love life looking good?

t_ch: 居然问这个问题吗？我…我是不是该闭上眼哩。
t_en: You're actually asking that? Should I... cover my eyes or something?

t_ch: 指向了「是」，社长大人的缘分要到了了吗？
t_en: It's pointing to "Yes"! Is your true love finally on the way, President?

t_ch: 请问我和TA认识吗？
t_en: Do I know them already?

t_ch: 请问TA就在我身边吗？
t_en: Are they right here with me?

t_ch: 你突然福至心灵，和尤狸安四目相对……
t_en: You have a sudden flash of insight and lock eyes with Joolyon...

t_ch: 啊啊啊啊社长大人请不要再问了哩！我只是像热爱工作一样热爱着社长哩！
t_en: AHHH! President, please, no more questions! I love you just like I love my work, okay!?

t_ch: 要不…尤狸安你来问？
t_en: Why don't you give it a try, Joolyon?

t_ch: 我我我，我吗…好吧，既然社长大人这样说了。
t_en: M-Me? You want me to ask? Well, if you insist, President.

t_ch: 胡构狸，你不要再装神弄鬼了，快快显身，否则我会立刻联系罗浮杂俎的大网红@小桂子Guinevere 来抓你！
t_en: Furbricato, quit the ghost act and show yourself! Otherwise, I'm calling the top Ghostly Grove influencer, @LilGuiGuinevere, to come and bust you!

t_ch: 别放狠话了，快提问！
t_en: Enough with the tough talk, just ask already!

t_ch: 胡构狸，胡构狸，你到底想做什么？有什么事就冲我来，不许伤害社长大人！
t_en: Furbricato, Furbricato, what's your deal anyway? If you want a piece of someone, come at me! Just leave the Boss out of this!

t_ch: O-K，「OK」？！你还真的冲我来哩！
t_en: O-K... "OK"!? Wait, you're actually coming for me!?

t_ch: 我要先下手为强哩，社长大人，请您保护好我，让我把报纸掀开看看！
t_en: I'm not waiting around for you! President, cover me! I'm gonna flip this newspaper over and see who's behind this!

t_ch: 啊～～且慢～～你们终、于发现我了～～
t_en: Ahhh~ Wait up~ You guys finally found me~

t_ch: 这下～～不得不现、形哩～～
t_en: Guess I have no choice... but to show myself now~

t_ch: 啊啊～～呵呵～～太久不出现，竟然现形失败了～～
t_en: Ahh~ Haha~ It's been so long since I've manifested that I messed it up~

t_ch: 闭上眼睛～～再来一次哩～～
t_en: Close your eyes~ Let me try that one more time~

t_ch: 在下是～～《狸狸周刊》隐藏员工：「胡构狸」！参见～～
t_en: I am~ Furbricato, the hidden employee of Furbobo Weekly! Nice to meet ya~

t_ch: 胡构狸，竟然是一只狸猫？
t_en: Wait, Furbricato is a furbo?

t_ch: 小巧思嘛～～我们狸猫一族就是这么起名的哩～～
t_en: Just a pun~ That's how we furbos come up with our names~

t_ch: 隐藏员工，这么个隐藏啊？
t_en: A "hidden" employee? What's that supposed to mean?

t_ch: 我不是刻意隐藏的～～我一直住在杂志社里～～只是从来没有狸看到过我哩～～
t_en: I wasn't hiding on purpose~ I've always lived here in the office, but no furbo ever noticed me~

t_ch: 那么，那张旧报纸，其实根本不是什么通灵感应，是你「隐藏」起来干的好事哩？
t_en: So that old newspaper wasn't some psychic phenomenon, but your "hidden" work?

t_ch: 嘿嘿～～是说报纸上会移动的尘灵吗～～它是从我胡构狸身上散落的～～当然听我的话哩～～
t_en: Hehe~ You mean those little wispae moving around on the paper? They're actually from my own body~ so obviously they do what I tell them~

t_ch: 我胡构狸啊～～从小就被爷爷说擅长胡编乱造～～才给我起的这个名字哩～～
t_en: My gramps always said I was a natural at fabricating stuff, so that's how I got my name~

t_ch: 从今天起～～我就是《狸狸周刊》的正式公开员工了～～
t_en: As of today, I'm officially stepping out of the shadows as a full-time employee of Furbobo Weekly~

t_ch: 这是胡构的一部分吗？
t_en: Is this just another one of your fabrications?

t_ch: 这不是胡构，是实情哩～～我是这里资历最老的员工，从绘世大人开天辟地的时候就是《狸狸周刊》的一员了哩～～
t_en: Of course not, it's the real deal~ I'm the OG employee here! I've been part of Furbobo Weekly since the great Graphia first painted the world into existence~

t_ch: 大胆！把我这个老板放在哪里？
t_en: Excuse me!? Where does that leave me? I'm the boss!

t_ch: 啊啊～～如果老板想给我发正式带合同编制五险一金的聘用书的话～～我也会欢欢喜喜地接受哩～～
t_en: Ahhh~ If the boss wants to hand over a real employment contract with health insurance and the whole nine yards, I'd be happy to take it~

t_ch: 从前的我～～用尽了各种办法～～都没能让这些狸猫们注意到我～～
t_en: Before this, I tried everything~ to get those furbos to notice me~

t_ch: 反而变成了《狸狸周刊》深夜闹鬼的怪谈～～
t_en: But I just ended up becoming a local ghost story about Furbobo Weekly being haunted at night~

t_ch: 直到{NICKNAME}到来～～发现了我的存在～～在我心里～～您就是我的「主人」哩～～
t_en: Until {NICKNAME} showed up and saw me for who I am~ From now on, you're the only "master" for me~

t_ch: 主人～～请狠狠地收下我胡构狸吧～～
t_en: Master! Please take me in and let me serve you~

t_ch: 这就是雏鸟情结吗？好可怕的黏人狸！不过《狸狸周刊》招聘新员工都是要面试的，目前还没有破格录取的先例哩……
t_en: Is this some kind of imprinting? This furbo is way too clingy! Look, Furbobo Weekly has an interview process for a reason. We can't just go around making exceptions...

t_ch: 我有些问题想问你…

t_ch: 我该怎么称呼你？

t_ch: 可惜我这样的人，注定无法感受你们的平凡与快乐，我要背负的是这满屋子的孤独。

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。

t_ch: 那还是不打扰你了。

t_ch: 可惜我还是喜欢这里——这里很黑，所以很安全……
t_en: Too bad I still like it in here — it's dark, so I feel safe...

t_ch: 阿德勒

t_ch: 你在找什么吗？

t_ch: 啊！

t_ch: 看你的服饰，也不是界种科的大人。算了算了，还是不和你说了。
t_en: Judging from your outfit, you don't look like you are a big shot from the Department of Ecology. Anyway, I better not talk about this with you.

t_ch: 你在说什么呢？
t_en: What are you talking about?

t_ch: 当然是关于珍稀生物……
t_en: Of course it's about a rare organism...

t_ch: 不对，你是谁？我好像没有见过你。
t_en: Hold on, who are you? I don't think I've seen you before.

t_ch: 看你的样子，也不是界种科的人。算了算了，还是不和你说了。
t_en: You don't look like you are from the Department of Ecology. Anyway, I better not talk about this with you.

t_ch: 你在研究什么生物呢？
t_en: What creatures do you study?

t_ch: 当然还是在围绕珍稀物种领域啦。那些小家伙们可比你们这种大人有意思多了！
t_en: Rare species of course. Those little creatures are so much more interesting than human grown-ups!

t_ch: 不过……看你的眼神，似乎很好奇的样子。这种眼神可在大人里不多见了。
t_en: However... the curiosity in your eyes... I don't see that a lot in grown-ups' eyes.

t_ch: 我叫阿德勒，是界种科室的科员。你有什么想问的，就尽管问吧。只要是我知道的，都会为你解答的。
t_en: I am Adler, a researcher from the Department of Ecology. Ask me if you have any questions. I'll answer anything I know.

t_ch: 你最喜欢什么珍稀物种？
t_en: What is your favorite rare species?

t_ch: 作为研究者，我对于珍稀物种都是平等看待的，不会有喜恶之别。
t_en: As a researcher, I treat every rare species equally. I don't have a preference.

t_ch: 但是从个人角度的话……我最喜欢的是呜呜伯。
t_en: But from my personal perspective... I'd say Wubbaboos are my favorite.

t_ch: 虽然它们有些调皮，但其实做事很有逻辑，还很重情感。
t_en: Although they can be mischievous sometimes, they are very logical and they value relationships a lot.

t_ch: 比空间站的有些大人，要好多了……
t_en: They are so much better than the grown-ups in the space station...

t_ch: 为什么会选择研究珍稀物种？
t_en: What made you study rare species?

t_ch: 珍稀物种的表达方式与思考逻辑，与成年人类是不相同的。
t_en: Their ways of expression and thinking are very different from human grown-ups.

t_ch: 但是不可否认，它们的处事方式与行为动机，有时候却往往比人类更有逻辑。
t_en: But their ways of handling things and the motives behind their behaviors are undeniably often more logical than that of the humans'.

t_ch: 大概就是这种逻辑感，让我更愿意研究它们吧。
t_en: It's probably their sense of logic that makes me want to study them.

t_ch: 你能听懂珍稀物种说话吗？
t_en: Can you understand their language?

t_ch: 那是当然。
t_en: Of course.

t_ch: 别看我是个小孩子，我可是能听懂许多珍稀物种的语言的！
t_en: You might think I am a kid, but I understand many languages spoken by rare species!

t_ch: 它们的语言习惯和我们不是同一个维度，经常一个音节就能表示很多意思。
t_en: Their languages are not in the same dimension as ours. They use one syllable to express many different meanings.

t_ch: 比如呜呜伯的短呜和长呜，就是截然不同的含义。
t_en: For example, Wubbaboo's short and long whines mean completely different things.

t_ch: 如果你有兴趣，可以加入界种科，进行深度学习。
t_en: You can join the Department of Ecology to study it if you are interested.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all questions I have.

t_ch: 如果以后有珍稀物种相关的事件，可以告诉我哦。
t_en: Please keep me posted if you find out anything about rare species.

t_ch: 辛克尔
t_en: Hinkel

t_ch: 物资调配尽可能快速，不要浪费一丝人力或者物力。
t_en: To not waste any personnel or resources, let's allocate our supplies as quickly as possible.

t_ch: 我需要尽可能快地拿到奇物相关的损伤判定资料，最全面的那种。
t_en: I need a comprehensive damage evaluation of the Curios as soon as possible.

t_ch: ……阁下，从刚刚开始，你好像就一直在注视着我指挥黑塔的重建复兴工作。请问是有什么事么？
t_en: ...With all due respect, you've been watching me for a while as I command the reconstruction and revival of the space station. How may I help you?

t_ch: 请问你是负责空间站复兴的工作人员吗？
t_en: Are you responsible for the space station revival?

t_ch: 我是界种星域科室室长辛克尔，目前负责灾后复兴工作以及安全区域的开辟。
t_en: I'm Hinkel, head of the Department of Ecology. I'm currently in charge of post-disaster recovery and the establishment of safe zones.

t_ch: 请无关人员迅速离开，不要妨碍我们工作。
t_en: If you are an outsider, you should leave. Do not interfere with our work.

t_ch: 这里还有多久可以恢复原样？
t_en: How long will it take to complete the restoration?

t_ch: 具体时间很难说，但我们会保证尽快恢复空间站的正常运行。
t_en: It's hard to say, but we promise that the space station will resume normal operation as soon as possible.

t_ch: 目前安全区域的开辟进程较慢，那里有许多军团的余孽盘踞在废墟之中。
t_en: Currently, the progress of establishing safe zones is slow. Remnants of the Legion are still entrenched in the ruins.

t_ch: 还希望你可以不要前往那边，以免发生意外。
t_en: Please don't go there in case it gets dangerous.

t_ch: 我还有一些问题想要咨询…
t_en: I have a few more questions for you...

t_ch: 我有什么可以帮你的吗？
t_en: Is there anything I can help you with?

t_ch: 很遗憾，没有。
t_en: I'm afraid not.

t_ch: 你作为星穹列车的宾客，应该待在更加安全的区域。
t_en: As a guest from the Astral Express, you should stay in a safer area.

t_ch: 这份工作非常危险，如果你出了什么问题，我可没有办法给艾丝妲交代。
t_en: This job is very dangerous. If anything happens to you, I really don't know how I would explain it to Asta.

t_ch: 安全区的开辟工作很危险吧？
t_en: It must be dangerous to establish safe zones, right?

t_ch: 军团的余孽凭借那里错综复杂的路径，隐匿起身。
t_en: Remnants of the Legion are taking advantage of the complex terrain there to hide in.

t_ch: 稍不留意，就有可能被它们偷袭伏击。
t_en: You might get ambushed if you are not careful.

t_ch: 不过我们会在安全的基础上进行开辟，不会急于求成，这点你可以放心。
t_en: However, we will proceed with safety as our top priority. We are not in a rush. Don't worry.

t_ch: 灾后复兴工作，主要是围绕哪几个方面？
t_en: What are the main aspects of the work regarding post-disaster recovery?

t_ch: 除开基础建设方面的维护，最主要的方面还是科员的心理健康。
t_en: Apart from infrastructure maintenance, the most important aspect is the staff's mental health.

t_ch: 纵然灾难会毁灭物质，但是那些都有机会复原。唯有人心的损伤，是难以弥补的。
t_en: Disasters are physically destructive, but the buildings can be rebuilt. It is the human heart that is irreparable.

t_ch: 你辛苦了，我没有什么想要问的了。
t_en: Thank you for your hard work. I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 感谢你的理解，希望你远离还未开辟的受灾区域，注意自身安全。
t_en: Thank you for your understanding. Please stay away from the affected areas that have not opened up yet. Stay safe.

t_ch: 你在做什么？
t_en: What are you doing?

t_ch: 我正在汇报法政科相关的工作。如您所见，我是法政科的向导，您可以叫我希拉。
t_en: I am reporting on my work in the Department of Galactic Geopolitics. As you can see, I'm a guide in the department. You can call me Sheila.

t_ch: 如果您需要什么帮助，也可以向我咨询。
t_en: If you need any help, please feel free to consult me.

t_ch: 你好像很忙的样子。
t_en: You seem very busy.

t_ch: 入侵军团造成的影响很大，整个空间站还未能恢复正常运行。
t_en: The Legion invasion has been very destructive. The space station has not been able to resume normal operation.

t_ch: 作为法政科的向导，我的工作自然也会比平日要繁琐一些。
t_en: As a guide of the Department of Galactic Geopolitics, I am even busier than usual.

t_ch: 我还有一些事情想要问你…
t_en: I've got something to ask you.

t_ch: 法政科会采取什么行动？
t_en: What action will the Department of Galactic Geopolitics take?

t_ch: 我只是法政科的一位向导而已，具体的方针策略，还得等创始人古恩老师决定。
t_en: I'm just a guide. Specific policies and strategies will be decided by the founder, Mr. Gunn.

t_ch: 但我相信，政法科一定会竭尽自己的努力，去弥补这场灾难导致的诸多漏洞。
t_en: But I believe the Department of Galactic Geopolitics will certainly try its best to make up for this disaster.

t_ch: 如果您感觉心理难受，我可以帮助您缓解一些压力。这也是我能为空间站做出的微薄贡献。
t_en: If you are not well mentally, I can help you relieve some stress. This is the least I can do for the space station.

t_ch: 你平日里也会给大家做心理疏导么？
t_en: Do you usually do psychological counseling for everyone?

t_ch: 在日常生活中，我更喜欢做一些思想实验。你可以理解为一些短问题测试。
t_en: Normally I prefer to do thought experiments, such as short quiz questions.

t_ch: 这些思想实验可以锻炼大家的思维，同时也能让我更加了解大家，与科员们相处。
t_en: Thought experiments can exercise people's mental capacity and help me understand them. It helps me to get along with the researchers.

t_ch: 等之后危机解除，你不妨也试一试？
t_en: When the crisis passes, would you like to have a try?

t_ch: 关于那位古恩老师……
t_en: About Mr. Gunn...

t_ch: 古恩老师是法政科的创始人，他富有智慧与哲思。
t_en: Mr. Gunn is the founder of the Department of Galactic Geopolitics. He is very wise and philosophical.

t_ch: 你可以与他多多交流沟通，想必会收获很多有趣的知识。
t_en: I am sure you will gain a lot of interesting knowledge if you talk to him more.

t_ch: 尤其是愚人戏方面，很难找到比他更懂的行家了。
t_en: Especially about Fool's Play. I don't think anyone will know more about it than him.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all questions I have.

t_ch: 能够解答您心中的困扰，我很荣幸。之后有需要，可以随时来与我联系。
t_en: It's my honor to answer your questions. Please contact me anytime if you have any further inquiries.

t_ch: 今天的湿度还不错，比较适合散步。
t_en: The humidity is just right today. It's perfect for a walk.

t_ch: 嗯……或许是应该换一种口味的午餐了。
t_en: Hmm... Maybe it's time to try something else for lunch.

t_ch: 那边的年轻人，你有什么推荐的菜品吗？
t_en: Youngster, do you have any recommendations?

t_ch: 你在对我说话吗？
t_en: Are you talking to me?

t_ch: 当然，年轻人。我们好像是第一次见？
t_en: Of course. I don't think we have met before, youngster.

t_ch: 没关系，一回生二回熟。看你的样子，应该是刚来这里不久吧？我叫古恩，已经是个老家伙啦。
t_en: Nevermind. You've not been here long, have you? I am Gunn. I am already an old guy.

t_ch: 年轻人，空间站的奥秘与趣味很多，还得你自己多多探索，才能感受。
t_en: Youngster, there are many mysteries and interesting things in the space station. You have to explore it yourself.

t_ch: 我们…好像不认识。
t_en: I... don't think we know each other.

t_ch: 不认识也没有关系，年轻人的口味，我还是想尝一尝的。
t_en: It doesn't matter. I would like to try what young people eat these days.

t_ch: 如果有推荐的菜品，可以告诉我；如果你有什么不解的，也可以试着问问我。
t_en: Let me know if you have any dishes you can recommend. Please feel free to ask me if you have any questions.

t_ch: 不过请别误会，我不是好为人师，只是喜欢和年轻人聊天罢了。
t_en: Please don't get me wrong. I'm not trying to lecture you. I simply enjoy talking to young people.

t_ch: 我还正好有一些问题…
t_en: I happen to have a few questions...

t_ch: 你来这里多久了？
t_en: How long have you been here?

t_ch: 具体的时间？记不清啦。
t_en: I don't remember exactly how long.

t_ch: 如你所见，我已经是一副老骨头了。
t_en: As you can see, I've got one foot in the grave.

t_ch: 在这里的时间转瞬即逝，对我而言，每一天都好像刚刚来到这里一样。
t_en: Time flies here. But for me, every day still feels like the first.

t_ch: 你是什么科室的研究员。
t_en: Which department are you from?

t_ch: 我和那位希拉女士是同一个科室的科员——法政科。
t_en: I came from the same department as Ms. Sheila, the Department of Galactic Geopolitics.

t_ch: 人老了，对于正义的探索已经比如年轻时那样热烈了。
t_en: I am old. My passion for justice is slowly fading away.

t_ch: 不过如果有年轻人想要与我交流一二，我还是很乐意聊聊的。
t_en: But if any young people fancy a discussion with me, I am more than happy to do that.

t_ch: 这场灾难，对你有影响吗？
t_en: Does this disaster affect you?

t_ch: 孩子，这是一场灾难，如你所言。
t_en: Kid, as you said, this is a disaster.

t_ch: 来到空间站的人，本就有一些是失去了家园的流离之辈。空间站对于他们而言，就是第二个家园。
t_en: Some people came to the space station because they lost their homes. The space station is like their second home.

t_ch: 不过幸好，空间站并没有失守，大家还能在这里，做实验，聊天，散步。
t_en: Fortunately, the space station survived. We can still do experiments, chat and stroll around.

t_ch: 真好，真好啊。
t_en: Good. It's really good.

t_ch: 我没有问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 好的孩子，以后有空，可以多多来聊聊。
t_en: Good. Kid, come and chat with me if you have time.

t_ch: 如果你对愚人戏有兴趣的话，就最好不过——我可知道不少有趣的戏呢！
t_en: If you are interested in Fool's Play, then you've come to the right person. I know many interesting ones!

t_ch: 一切都如星神密卷中所言，一点不假，一点不差……
t_en: Everything happens exactly as predicted in the Mystic Scroll of Aeons, exactly...

t_ch: 若果真如此，那么岂不是那位大人曾注视…不，是正在注视着……
t_en: If this is true, the lord must've seen... No, must be watching...

t_ch: 天哪，我快要呼吸不过来了！天哪！
t_en: Gosh, I can't breathe! Oh my gosh!

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: Are you all right?

t_ch: 好？我当然是好！甚至说是好极了！
t_en: Okay? I am good! I mean, I am great!

t_ch: 好这个词语，已经无法形容我此时此刻的心境！
t_en: Good is an understatement for what I'm feeling right now!

t_ch: 若你沐浴阳光的温暖时，有幸瞥见太阳的雄姿，你会是什么感受？！
t_en: How would you feel if you get to see the sun whilst basking in its warmth?

t_ch: 就是我此时此刻的感受！
t_en: That's exactly how I feel right now!

t_ch: 看你的样子，需要去医疗舱。
t_en: I think you need to go to the Medical Cabin.

t_ch: 医疗舱？不，我不需要。请你相信，我对我自己的身体状况非常清楚。
t_en: Medical Cabin? No, I don't think so. Trust me, I know my body just fine.

t_ch: 我不过是有些太过幸福，所以导致呼吸失常罢了。
t_en: I just got too carried away, making me a bit out of breath.

t_ch: 看，深呼吸，像是我这样，吸气…呼气…没错，这样就能调节好了。
t_en: Take a deep breath. Inhale... exhale... right, this is how I adjust my breathing.

t_ch: 奇怪，怎么还是觉得心跳加速？看来这招不好用啊。
t_en: How strange, my heartbeat is still very fast. It doesn't seem to be working.

t_ch: 我有些事情想问你…
t_en: I have a few questions for you...

t_ch: 是…哪个领域方面的知识？我不是通识类学者……
t_en: In what field of study? I am not a general scholar...

t_ch: 如果我回答不上来，还是请你去询问别的科员吧。
t_en: If I can't answer your questions, please ask the other researchers.

t_ch: 你说的那位大人是谁？
t_en: Who is the lord you were talking about?

t_ch: 哦！你也是对这次入侵事件感兴趣的科员吗？这件事可有来头了！
t_en: Oh! Are you also interested in the invasion? That was a huge event!

t_ch: 这次军团中的敌人，是反物质融铸而成的银河兵器——你知道这意味着什么吗？
t_en: The enemies in the Legion are cosmic weapons made of antimatter. Do you know what that means?

t_ch: 这正是对应了星神密卷里的痴缠与灾影啊！因此，我得出结论……
t_en: It echos the infatuation and disaster in the Mystic Scroll of Aeons! As a result, I've come to this conclusion...

t_ch: 这些入侵者，就是「毁灭」纳努克的追随者！绝不会有错！
t_en: These invaders are followers of Nanook the Destruction! This can't be wrong!

t_ch: 星神密卷是什么？
t_en: What is the Mystic Scroll of Aeons?

t_ch: 这就说来话长了。你可以理解为对于星神相关的文化记载。
t_en: It's a long story. You can say it's a record of cultures related to Aeons.

t_ch: 里面的遣词造句都非常晦涩难懂，我没日没夜地研读，也只能窥见其中一隅的哲思。
t_en: It is written in a very obscure fashion. Even though I've spent countless days and nights studying it, I can only understand a fraction of its philosophy.

t_ch: 不过有一点很重要，那就是星神密卷的内容，一定是根据现实撰写，而非是凭空捏造。
t_en: But one very important thing is that the content of the Mystic Scroll of Aeons is based on facts, not groundless fabrications.

t_ch: 这次的军团就是最好的证明！
t_en: This Legion is the best way to prove this!

t_ch: 你有见过那位大人吗？
t_en: Have you seen that lord?

t_ch: 见？！你在说些什么呢！
t_en: Have I seen that lord? What are you talking about?

t_ch: 古时候的人们不知晓有太阳，但却也知道崇拜太阳神。对他们而言，亲身体会太阳的灼热，是太过热烈的体验。
t_en: In ancient times, people were not aware of the exact nature of the sun but they still worshiped the Sun God. For them, the idea of approaching the scorching sun is too much for them to handle.

t_ch: 我能从现实中，考究到星神存在的蛛丝马迹，就已经很满足了。
t_en: I am already happy that I managed to find clues about the existence of Aeons.

t_ch: 如…如果还能被星神大人所注视的话……嘿嘿……
t_en: If... If I am really being watched by the great Aeons... Heheh...

t_ch: 我应该没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't think I have anything to ask.

t_ch: 如果你了解到其他与星神相关的线索，请记得与我联系！
t_en: Please contact me if you have any other clues about the Aeons!

t_ch: 最好是第一时间，只与我一个人联系！
t_en: Preferably right away, and only me!

t_ch: ……如此奇物，四件缺了三件，何时才能。
t_en: ...Three out of the four Curios are missing. When will we...

t_ch: 小友莫不是{NICKNAME}？想必你也是受到「复兴宣言」感召，前来应征奇物收容志愿者。
t_en: If it isn't the infamous {NICKNAME}? Surely you are here to volunteer in the Curio Collection Squad, inspired by the Declaration of Revival.

t_ch: 原来是温明德，温队长。
t_en: So, you must be Wen Mingde.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}青年才俊，久仰久仰。明德有失远迎，切莫怪罪。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I have heard good things about you. Please excuse me for this informal reception.

t_ch: 我只是从此处路过。
t_en: I am just passing by.

t_ch: 相逢就是缘。更别提小友青年才俊，此段对话，是你我之间命中注定。
t_en: It's our destiny to meet. For a talented young person like yourself, this conversation is meant to be...

t_ch: 容我重新介绍下自己。在下温明德，是「黑塔•万有应物科」负责人，目前兼任奇物再收容小队队长，统筹基座舱段的安全区开辟工作。
t_en: Please allow me to introduce myself again. I am Wen Mingde, head of the Herta Department of Implement Arts. I am also the Curio Collection Squad Leader, in charge of establishing safe areas in the base zone.

t_ch: 不瞒小友，方才明德正在为奇物收容小队的人手苦恼。
t_en: Allow me to be frank. I am currently troubled by the lack of human resources available for the Curio Collection Squad.

t_ch: 空间站共计四方舱段，如今人手却只堪堪顾得上其中一隅，如此下去，何日才能达成「复兴宣言」所立宏远之计。
t_en: There are four zones in the space station. With our current capacity, we can only take care of one. If the situation persists, I wonder when we will achieve the objective we have set out in the Declaration of Revival.

t_ch: 明德敛气定心，细细思索一番，方才领悟容纳编外成员才是正道。
t_en: After calming myself down and giving it some thought, I realized that recruiting external members may be the solution.

t_ch: 正在此时，小友便与我搭话，你说这是巧也不巧。
t_en: While I was lingering on that thought, you began a conversation with me. What a pleasant coincidence.

t_ch: 小友若是还有任何想了解的事情，也可以问我。
t_en: If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to let me know.

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I want to ask...

t_ch: 黑塔•万有应物科？
t_en: Herta Department of Implement Arts?

t_ch: 哈，应物科乃是黑塔的重要科室之一，包揽了站内所有资产的流入流出、盘查与清点。
t_en: Ha! An important department within the space station, Implement Arts is. We oversee the incoming and outgoing of all our assets, including inventory checks and counts.

t_ch: 并且，我们与「星际和平公司」保持着极为密切的交流。若你行走银河，耳目宽广，多少应该听过我们的名号。
t_en: Furthermore, we maintain close communications with Interastral Peace Corporation. For someone as well-traveled as yourself, I'm sure you've heard of our reputation.

t_ch: 奇物再收容小队？
t_en: Curio Collection Squad?

t_ch: 奇物再收容小队嘛，是空间站依据灾后复兴需要，临时牵头成立的应急小组。
t_en: The Curio Collection Squad is a temporary emergency unit established by the space station to address the needs of the post-disaster revival.

t_ch: 顾名思义，主要负责回收因怪物入侵而收容失效的奇物。
t_en: As the name suggests, it is mainly responsible for collecting the Curios that have been displaced due to the recent monster invasion.

t_ch: 安全区？
t_en: Safe zone?

t_ch: 就是你我二人脚下站定的位置，黑塔科员向「反物质军团」组织反击的据点。
t_en: It is the very ground on which we stand. The stronghold from which the space station researchers fought back against the Antimatter Legion.

t_ch: 虽说空间站已大体受控，但怪物还未被全数清除。
t_en: Although the situation is generally under control, the monsters have not been completely wiped out.

t_ch: 安全区就是过渡计划的产物，作为我们逐步夺回的舱段失地，最终将如星星之火，连为一体。
t_en: The safe zone is the result of our transition plan, for us to take back all the lost zones. I am confident all the dots will eventually be connected.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 无妨，小友遇到任何困难，再来找明德便是。
t_en: Good. For any questions you may encounter in the future, my young friend, please do not hesitate to come to me.

t_ch: 日后，明德也还要多多仰仗小友。
t_en: In the future, there is much I must rely on you for.

t_ch: 无论是来自裂缝深渊中的敌人，还是来自不轨人心的算计。
t_en: Whether they are enemies from the abyss or people with ill intentions...

t_ch: 只要我还在空间站一日，就不会允许那些孽障在黑塔大人的地盘肆意撒野！
t_en: So long as I'm in the space station, I will never let them ravage the territory of Madam Herta!

t_ch: 倘若非要凌踏这片科学的净土，那么就先从我，卡波特身上跨过去吧！
t_en: If they are to trample on this pure land of science, they'll have to go through me first!
